contract for bridging ERC-20 between chains using an ERC-7786 gateway. In order to use this contract, two functions must be implemented to link it to the token: * {_onSend}: called when a crosschain transfer is going out. Must take the sender tokens or revert. * {_onReceive}: called when a crosschain transfer is coming in. Must give tokens to the receiver. This base contract is used by the {BridgeERC20}, which interfaces with legacy ERC-20 tokens, and {BridgeERC7802}, which interface with ERC-7802 to provide an approve-free user experience. It is also used by the {ERC20Crosschain} extension, which embeds the bridge logic directly in the token contract.","errors":{"ERC7786RecipientUnauthorizedGateway(address,bytes)":[{"details":"Error thrown if the gateway is not authorized to send messages to this contract on behalf of the sender."}],"LinkAlreadyRegistered(bytes)":[{"details":"Reverted when trying to register a link for a chain that is already registered. Note: the `chain` argument is a \"chain-only\" InteroperableAddress (empty address)."}],"SafeCastOverflowedUintDowncast(uint8,uint256)":[{"details":"Value doesn't fit in a uint of `bits` size."}]},"events":{"LinkRegistered(address,bytes)":{"details":"Emitted when a new link is registered. crosschainTransfer(bytes,uint256).details: Transfer `amount` tokens to a crosschain receiver. Note: The `to` parameter is the full InteroperableAddress (chain ref + address).

getLink(bytes).details: Returns the ERC-7786 gateway used for sending and receiving cross-chain messages to a given chain. Note: The `chain` parameter is a "chain-only" InteroperableAddress (empty address) and the `counterpart` returns the full InteroperableAddress (chain ref + address) that is on `chain`.

receiveMessage(bytes32,bytes,bytes).details: Endpoint for receiving cross-chain message. This function may be called directly by the gateway.
